mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate labor income, which drives consumption and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months with positive PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation exceeds 5 percent on a year-over-year basis, the Federal Reserve is compelled to accelerate monetary tightening, raising equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 5.0% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5 percent combined with an unemployment rate below 4 percent signals aggressive monetary tightening into a tight labor market, which compresses profit margins via wage costs and increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the interaction term of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is positive or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal that elevated borrowing costs are restraining manufacturing activity and supply, amplifying equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In walk-forward data, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO declines during identified tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in monthly housing starts indicate weakening residential construction and durable goods demand, which reduces expected cyclical revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during months with declining HOUST over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below zero percent signals an aggregate demand shortfall that validates recession expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is negative during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of CPI inflation above 5 percent year-over-year and a month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates stagflation, which compresses consumer discretionary earnings and raises real discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and RSAFS does not produce a statistically negative Rank IC (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor demand and recession, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months.
